honest-type/src/honest_type/reserved.py:: equivalent: removing the trailing `return None` of reservation_layer leaves the function falling off its end, which returns None — the same result.
honest-type/src/honest_type/types.py:Fal: equivalent: `total=False` on a TypedDict is a static-typing directive with no runtime effect, so flipping it to True cannot change any value the suite observes.
honest-type/src/honest_type/boundary.py:: equivalent: emptying the policy KEY leaves the reason unlisted, and an unlisted reason defaults to 'fault' (blocks) via rules.get(reason, 'fault') — the same outcome as the explicit 'fault' value.
equivalent: emptying the policy KEY leaves the reason unlisted, which defaults to 'fault' (blocks) — the same outcome as the explicit value.
equivalent: emptying the policy KEY leaves the reason unlisted, which defaults to 'fault' (blocks) — the same outcome as the explicit value.
equivalent: only the value 'fault' blocks; any other value (including '') warns, so emptying 'warn' does not change the block/pass outcome.
equivalent: only the value 'fault' blocks; any other value (including '') warns, so emptying 'warn' does not change the block/pass outcome.
equivalent: swapping one fault-keyed reason for another sibling leaves both mapped to 'fault', and the displaced reason defaults to 'fault' — every reason still blocks identically.

equivalent: the exception path calls server_default directly; _out_server reads only failure['code'], never the category, so emptying the category 'server' changes no output.
equivalent: removing the 'server' key makes .get('server', server_default) fall to its default, which is server_default — the same handler.
equivalent: collapsing to a single 'client' key still resolves a server category to the server_default fallback and a client category to client_default — identical routing.
honest-type/src/honest_type/state_machin: equivalent: no valid states/events declaration distinguishes `and` from `or` — a plain set has neither .get nor base_types (both False) and a vocabulary has both (both True); only an invalid dict-without-base_types would differ, which is not a valid declaration.
honest-type/src/honest_type/classify.py:: equivalent: this add() fires only when the captured token is PRESENT (capture_index is not None), so the type is already in ticket_index_by_type; phase 2d only flags a type missing when it is NOT in ticket_index, so claimed_types membership is irrelevant here — removing it changes no output.
honest-type/src/honest_type/vocabulary.p: equivalent: the catch-all corpus (_CATCH_ALL_SAMPLE) is a fixed, diverse sample whose exact characters, per-string length, and count are unobservable — a clear catch-all accepts ~all of it (ratio ~1.0) and a discriminating type accepts a small fraction, regardless of these constants, so the construction outcome is unchanged (confirmed by the catch_all_predicate law).

equivalent: `a<=b or b<=a` is symmetric; weakening one <= to < only differs when a==b, but then the other term (b<=a) is still True, so the subset/overlap result is unchanged.
equivalent: `a<=b or b<=a` is symmetric; weakening the second <= to < only differs when a==b, but then the first term (a<=b) is still True, so the result is unchanged.
equivalent: BaseException.__new__ already sets args=(message,), so str(exc) still returns the message without the explicit super().__init__(message) call — removing it changes no observable output.

equivalent: the _check_reserved skip for non-bounded recognizers is redundant — members() of a predicate recognizer is empty, so the reserved-word loop is a no-op for them (verified: a predicate vocabulary still constructs identically when the skip is removed).

equivalent: the _check_reserved skip for non-bounded recognizers is redundant — members() of a predicate recognizer is empty, so the reserved-word loop is a no-op for them (verified: a predicate vocabulary still constructs identically when the skip is removed).

equivalent: the _check_catch_all skip for bounded Sets is redundant — a realistic Set accepts a negligible fraction of the fixed corpus (far below 95%), so sampling it adds no rejection (verified: removing the skip changes no construction outcome).

equivalent: the _check_catch_all skip for bounded Sets is redundant — a realistic Set accepts a negligible fraction of the fixed corpus (far below 95%), so sampling it adds no rejection (verified: removing the skip changes no construction outcome).

equivalent: unreachable in merge — two valid vocabularies that merge (no name collision) cannot present a composed type referencing an unknown base: each composed already references only its own vocabulary's bases (which survive the merge), and two composed capturing the same base would require that base in both vocabularies, which is a name collision merge rejects first.

equivalent: unreachable in merge — two valid vocabularies that merge (no name collision) cannot present a cross-vocabulary capture ambiguity: each composed already references only its own vocabulary's bases (which survive the merge), and two composed capturing the same base would require that base in both vocabularies, which is a name collision merge rejects first.

equivalent: this is _accepts's `except: return False`; replacing it with pass returns None instead, but the only consumer is `sum(1 for t in corpus if _accepts(...))` where None and False are both falsy — the acceptance count is unchanged.

equivalent in practice: > vs >= differs only for a recognizer accepting exactly 95.0% (950/1000) of the fixed deterministic corpus; no constructible recognizer sits at that exact knife-edge, so the boundary is unobservable (a recognizer below or above 95% behaves identically under both).
